content:
At the core of any filter is Tiered Currency. On the Currency Tier List tab, you can see and change which Currency belongs to which Tier in detail. Here you have some shortcuts to easily hide common Currency, and which size of Gold stacks you want to hide & highlight.

You can also review or change your Tiering Mode here, which determines how Currency is divided into tiers.

Small and large stacks of Currency are promoted by one or two tiers, respectively. You can configure this behavior here.

Changes to rules are saved automatically, and immediately reflected in the Filter Output. You can always verify the output here.

Tip: Rule Navigation. CTRL-clicking on a rule's title will scroll to that rule in the Filter Output, and vice versa.

Tip: Item Simulator. Wondering why an item is shown or hidden in the game? Hover over it, press CTRL+C, then CTRL+V anywhere in the tool to see if and how it would be displayed, and by which rule(s).

After having picked a Preset, you can use the Quick Filters (and later Tier Lists) to fine-tune your filter.

The Quick Filters are divided into collapsible Sections with Groups of Rules. Rules from collapsed Sections and Groups still apply.

We save the toggle state of Sections and Groups. During this tutorial, we'll expand and collapse sections for you, but we'll restore them afterwards.

Hover over any (?) for more information about any rule (click to pin).

A button allows you to customize a rule in more detail.

Flask and Charm filters function the same as Equipment filters, but receive their own section here: specify which flasks and charms you're looking for in particular, and decide which general valuable flasks and charms you want to see, to trade or vendoror salvage.

Leveling Gear will only show items for your character, and only the best Base Types for the Area you're in. Magic and Rare items or items with at least the specified linked sockets will remain visible for longer.

Also configure which items you want to keep showing to vendor for Currencysalvage for Currency or sell for Gold.

Tip: Highlights & Tiers. Filter rules can Show, Hide or Highlight items. Highlighting means that an item will receive both a colored Beam and Minimap Icon.

The Highlight color reflects the item's tier, based on its value: White for D-Tier, Yellow for C-Tier, Orange for B-Tier, Red for A-Tier, all the way up to Purple for S-Tier.

You can configure these colors & styles later, on the Cosmetic tab.

As you advance through the campaign and through maps, you are only interested in Maps of a certain level and type.

Use the 'Static' setting to highlight Maps based on their color (White / Yellow / Red). With the 'Dynamic' setting, you can highlight Maps relative to which ones you're currently looking for. Finally, use the 'Endgame' setting to configure, in detail, which types of Endgame Maps you're interested in, and at what tier you want them to be highlighted.

Tip: Cosmetics. Notice how maps have their own distinct Minimap Icons. You can change this, and much more, on the Cosmetic tab!

For each of these rules, the filter can additionally highlight Variants, like Unidentified Tiered and Exceptional versions or other interesting Crafting Basesgood Crafting Bases, 6-Linked versions, or items with a lot of Memory Strands -- you can configure which ones you're interested in using the button. Have a look!

You'll notice that these Variants have an icon next to them. We use these icons throughout the Quick Filters. They tell you whether that type of item will be visible, always hidden, highlighted, or whether it depends on other rules (no icon). You can change the tier by clicking the icon, or on the Items Tier List tab.

Tip: My Equipment Tiers. Note that when you change the Equipment Tier of a certain Variant, you'll change it for all My Equipment rules of that type. For example, if you change the 'Regular Rare' Equipment Type for one 'My Weapon' rule to highlight it as Yellow Tier, 'Regular Rare' equipment from all 'My Equipment' rules will be highlighted as Yellow Tier.

The right-hand side of the Quick Filters tab deals with Equipment.

Later on, we'll look at generally valuable items, but in this section, you can select the weapons, armour and jewellery that your character uses, or you want to track in particular. This is especially useful if you're playing SSF! You can set a minimum Rarity, Gear Tier, Linked SocketsUnidentified Item Tier and Item Level, or Advanced Conditions.

Tip: Gear Tiers. Our tool classifies Gear Base Types into Basic, Advanced, Expert and Elite tiers. You can see and change which Base Type belongs to which Tier on the Gear Tier List tab, later.

Because you have enabled the League Start settings, we've disabled these rules; but you might still want to specify exceptions for items like Quivers and Caster Weapons.

We've already preselected the weapon types based on your chosen class. Go ahead and change it or add additional weapon types as needed.

Rules which have a gold line in front were automatically generated from your Class or Progress Preset, and might get changed along with presets.

Go ahead and set your character's weapon type(s) now!

In this section, you can configure which other potentially valuable or useful equipment to show or highlight; to trade or use for other characters: Influenced Gear, Special GearUnidentified Tiered Gear, Identified Items and Crafting Bases.
Here you can hide, show or highlight several other types of non-Currency items, like Quest items or League-specific items.

That's it, you've completed the tour of the Quick Filters tab!

What's next?

Review and fine-tune the tiers of Currency, Divination Cards, Items, Uniques, and more on the Tier Lists tab. Create fine-grained Custom Rules. Change how items will be displayed on the Cosmetic tab, and Preview how your filter will look in-game.
Here you can specify what to do with any Equipment which is not selected by any of the above filters. You can hide all remaining Gear; and decide at what Rarity to hide any remaining Jewellery.

Here you can specify which valuable Skill and Support Gems to highlight.

While leveling through the campaign, you might want to enter a list of 'My Gems' to highlight. You can use the 'Bulk Import' button, and either type or copy/paste and parse a list of gems from a build guide. Don't worry about other pasted text, we'll extract just the gems.

Go ahead and try it now!

Also Unique items -- or rather, their Base Types -- are divided into tiers based on their potential value. You have plenty of options to configure which ones you're interested in, and which ones you want to hide.

Tip: Hiding Uniques. Enabling these rules will highlight these uniques. Disabling them will, by default, not hide them: they will still get shown, just not highlighted. This is confirmed by the icon next to the Rule name. To hide any uniques from disabled rules, enable 'Hide All Remaining Uniques'. To confirm this, the tool will show a icon instead.

If you're just running maps and not doing any bosses, for instance, you can safely hide all 'Good Drop-Restricted Uniques'. Don't forget to press ALT whenever you do occasionally kill a boss!
